articles.title: Union Berlin make history with first female head coach
articles.summary: Marie-Louise Eta becomes the first woman appointed to manage a men's team in one of Europe's top five leagues as she is named interim head coach of Bundesliga side Union Berlin.

articles.title: Cup run can help Leeds in relegation fight - Farke
articles.summary: Leeds United head coach Daniel Farke dismisses suggestions the FA Cup could distract his side from their Premier League relegation battle, as they prepare to face Manchester United.

articles.title: Watch: Match of the Day
articles.summary: Highlights from five Premier League games as the season enters the final stretch.

articles.title: Tottenham will go down - Sutton
articles.summary: Football Focus pundits Chris Sutton and Steph Houghton discuss whether Roberto de Zerbi can improve Spurs and keep them in the Premier League.

articles.title: Eta becomes first female head coach in men's top five European leagues
articles.summary: Marie-Louise Eta has become the first female head coach in the men's top five European leagues after being announced as Union Berlin's new boss.

articles.title: 'Big punch in the face' - could Arsenal really blow title from here?
articles.summary: Nine points clear with six games to play - could Arsenal still really blow the Premier League title from that position?

articles.title: Scott nets the winner as Bournemouth stun Arsenal
articles.summary: Arsenal's title hopes take a hit as Bournemouth win 2-1 at the Emirates with goals from Junior Kroupi and Alex Scott.

articles.title: 'Weak, vulnerable, and very, very nervous' - Shearer on Arsenal
articles.summary: Match of the Day pundits Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy look at how a "nervous" Arsenal side dropped points in the title race with a 2-1 home defeat to Bournemouth.

articles.title: 'He brings an energy' - Should Ngumoha start against PSG?
articles.summary: Alan Shearer and Danny Murphy debate whether 17-year-old Rio Ngumoha should start for Liverpool in their Champions League quarter-final second leg against PSG, after starring and scoring in the Reds' ...

articles.title: Everything he does is impressive - Shearer lauds Igor Thiago
articles.summary: Match of the Day's Alan Shearer analyses the performance of Brentford's Igor Thiago, as the Brazilian striker took his Premier League tally to 21 goals with a brace in his side's 2-2 draw with Everton...

articles.title: Fulham 'cannot repeat' first-half display - Silva
articles.summary: Fulham manager Marco Silva criticises his sides first-half display, after conceding twice in four minutes against Liverpool.

articles.title: 'A big punch on the face' - Arteta
articles.summary: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta describes the 2-1 defeat at home to Bournemouth as "a big punch on the face" as they drop points in the Premier League title race.

articles.title: Rather Europe chase than relegation scrap - Moyes
articles.summary: Everton manager David Moyes says he's happier that his side are chasing a spot in Europe rather than fighting relegation, as they get a late equaliser against fellow European hopefuls Brentford.

articles.title: 'We don't want to talk about Europe' - Hurzeler
articles.summary: Brighton manager Fabian Hurzeler played down his sides chances of finishing in a European spot after they won a third straight game with a 2-0 victory over Burnley.

articles.title: Thiago in an 'amazing place' - Andrews
articles.summary: Brentford manager Keith Andrews praises his striker Igor Thiago as he scores another two goals during a 2-2 draw against Everton to bring his total to 21 for the season.

articles.title: Kent trail to Northants as Crawley continues to struggle
articles.summary: Zak Crawley made his third successive sub-30 County Championship score for Kent after Northamptonshire ran up a record 684 for two declared at Canterbury.
